title: Migrate public coordination API to session queue scoped leases
summary: Replace public claim vocabulary with session queue scoped leases while preserving a tested transitional alias for existing callers.

goal.outcome: CodeWiki public coordination language and API use session queue/scoped lease concepts, with legacy claim naming retained only as a documented transitional alias.
acceptance: Domain/session queue types model focus, waits, scoped leases, handoff metadata, and isolation context without public claim terminology.
New or renamed application tool/API surface exposes session queue scoped lease operations; legacy codewiki_claim remains as a compatibility alias with deprecation/transition documentation if needed.
Pi tool schemas, skills, UI labels, generated state summaries, and validation isolation fields use session queue/scoped lease language in public output.
Storage remains .codewiki/session/queue.json unless migration planning explicitly changes it.
Tests cover scoped lease create/list/release/heartbeat behavior and legacy alias compatibility.
non_goals: Do not break existing users abruptly by deleting codewiki_claim before compatibility alias and migration notes exist.
Do not treat session queue leases as durable roadmap truth.
verification: stale-term scan for public claim vocabulary with allowed compatibility exceptions only

delta.desired: Session queue scoped leases are the canonical project lexicon and source/API concept for parallel coordination.
delta.current: Implementation and Pi tool names still use change claim terminology while docs now mark claim as transitional.
delta.closure: Public API/output/skills use session queue scoped leases, legacy alias is tested, and stale public claim terms are controlled.
